Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion To Require To Provide Security
Off Calendar Sep-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Friday, September 19, 2014:
Martin Eng (415.823.2285), Plaintiff appearing in Propria Persona;
Michael Raifsnider, Esq. of Raifsnider Law Offices (415.398.9001) for Defendants.

Plaintiff provides a copy of his reply to defendants' opposition to PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion To Require To Provide Security to review.

The Court, having reviewed briefs submitted and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion To Require To Provide Security is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008, no new facts, law or circumstances. Order signed in open court.
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)

Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Martin Eng (415-823-2285), plaintiff/moving party In Propria Persona
Michael Raifsnider, Esq. (415-308-9001), counsel for defendant/opposing party

Court hears arguments from both sides regarding the motion and regarding a notice of stay filed by Mr. Eng. Court finds this action is not stayed. PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion To Require To Provide Security is Continued to September 19, 2014 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 501. Notice is waived by both sides.

Notice Of Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant Martin Eng To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1,Et.Seq.
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Raifsnider, Esq. of Raisnider Law Offices, (415-398-9001), for Sherman Young, Sherwood Young and Lam Louise S.K., Defendants and moving parties.
No appearance by Plaintiff and opposing party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs SHERMAN YOUNG, SHERWOOD YOUNG and LAM, LOUISE S.K.'s Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant Martin Eng To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1 is GRANTED. Moving defendants have shown that Plaintiff has no reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits against the moving defendants. Plaintiff shall post a bond in the amount of $20,000 no later than August 22, 2014. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
